tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement by early April 2026, with a median of $71,247 (-0.5% from current spot), suggesting near-term consolidation. The 25th-75th percentile range of $70,172-$72,918 reflects tight consensus, though the 10th percentile at $70,000 indicates modest downside risk being priced in across 62 markets.

By month-end April 2026, markets are pricing a more meaningful rally to a median of $75,568 (+5.5% from spot), reflecting growing bullish conviction over the month. The wide range from $62,500 (10th percentile) to $80,000 (90th percentile) indicates substantial uncertainty, with the 75th percentile at $78,676 showing material upside scenarios are actively being traded.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets with $9.1M in volume expect WTI to decline 9% to a median of $86/bbl by end-April 2026, with a 50% probability range between $78-$124. The 10th-90th percentile spread ($72-$144) reflects substantial uncertainty, though the median leans toward moderate downside from current spot.

By end-June 2026, the lighter $969K volume market expects a steeper 18.3% decline to $77/bbl median, with the interquartile range at $69-$149 showing elevated tail risk on both sides. The wide dispersion and lower liquidity suggest elevated uncertainty two years out, with meaningful probability mass in both $60 downside and $188+ upside scenarios.
